contract.name: v2/award_spending/recipient.md
contract.description: Aggregates prime award obligations by recipient for a given fiscal year and awarding toptier agency, returning recipients ordered by total obligated amount.

awarding_agency_id.description: Identifier for the awarding toptier agency whose recipient spending should be summarized.
awarding_agency_id.constraints: Required. Must be an integer that maps to a valid awarding toptier agency; other integers return 400 and non-integer values trigger an HTML 500 response.

fiscal_year.description: Federal fiscal year to aggregate prime award obligations for.
fiscal_year.constraints: Required integer. Any integer is accepted, but years without data return empty results; non-integer input triggers an HTML 500 response.

limit.description: Maximum number of recipients to return per page.
limit.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 100 when omitted. Values ≤ 0 are normalized to 1, values > 500 are capped to 500, and non-integer input triggers an HTML 500 response.

page.description: 1-indexed page number of the recipient list to return.
page.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 1. Values ≤ 0 are normalized to 1, pages beyond the result set return empty results with 200 status, and non-integer input triggers an HTML 500 response.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata with normalized paging values and navigation links.

count.description: Total number of recipients matching the query across all pages.

page.description: Page number returned after server normalization.

has_next_page.description: True when additional pages can be requested.

has_previous_page.description: True when an earlier page exists.

next.description: Absolute URL for the next page when available, otherwise null.

current.description: Absolute URL reflecting the returned page with normalized query parameters.

previous.description: Absolute URL for the previous page when available, otherwise null.

results.description: Recipients sorted by obligated_amount descending. Entries include award category, aggregated obligated amount, and a recipient object with the display name.

award_category.description: Prime award category label. Observed values include 'contract', 'idv', 'grant', 'direct payment', and 'loans'.

obligated_amount.description: Aggregated obligated amount formatted as a decimal string; may be zero or negative for deobligations.

recipient_name.description: Display name for the recipient. May be a real name, the literal 'REDACTED DUE TO PII', or null when disclosure is restricted.

recipient.recipient_name: STATE OF CALIFORNIA WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD

body.detail: Awarding Agency ID provided must correspond to a toptier agency

quirks: limit defaults to 100, is floored to 1, and capped at 500; pagination URLs always echo the normalized value.
page defaults to 1; zero or negative inputs are normalized to 1, while oversized page requests return empty result arrays with a 200 status.
Results remain sorted by obligated_amount descending even when values are zero or negative, so later pages can contain only deobligations.
recipient.recipient_name may be a real name, the literal 'REDACTED DUE TO PII', or null depending on disclosure rules.
A current raw-MCP bench did not validate any stable upstream join source for awarding_agency_id; DoD autocomplete id 1173 returned an empty FY2025 dataset.
Non-integer query parameters trigger HTML 500 error pages instead of JSON validation errors.
risks: Clients must guard against HTML 500 responses when query parameters are not integers, otherwise JSON parsers will fail.
Large counts combined with the default limit=100 can return very large payloads; callers should set smaller limits when bandwidth is a concern.
Recipient names can be null or redacted, so downstream systems must tolerate missing or placeholder display names.
Loose fiscal_year validation means typos (e.g., far-future or negative years) silently return empty data, which can hide mistakes.
gaps: Additional award_category labels beyond the observed set (contract, idv, grant, direct payment, loans) may exist but were not verified.
A stable upstream raw endpoint for sourcing awarding_agency_id has not been documented or re-probed.
Rules determining when recipient_name is null versus 'REDACTED DUE TO PII' remain undocumented.
Supported fiscal_year range is unspecified; the API accepts any integer but returns empty data outside observed ranges.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Missing one or more required query parameters: fiscal_year, awarding_agency_id"}
{"detail":"Awarding Agency ID provided must correspond to a toptier agency"}

mismatches: Documentation describes awarding_agency_id as a recipient identifier, but the live API requires a valid awarding toptier agency id and rejects other integers.
Documentation implies award_category will be 'contracts', yet production responses use lower-case labels such as 'contract', 'idv', 'grant', 'direct payment', and 'loans'.
Documentation presents limit as an optional number without mentioning the default of 100, normalization to 1, or the hard cap of 500.
gaps: Additional award_category labels beyond the observed set (contract, idv, grant, direct payment, loans) may exist but were not verified.
Rules determining when recipient_name is null versus 'REDACTED DUE TO PII' remain undocumented.
Supported fiscal_year range is unspecified; the API accepts any integer but returns empty data outside observed ranges.
risks: Non-integer query inputs yield HTML 500 pages, so clients must sanitize parameters or handle non-JSON error payloads.
Defaulting to limit=100 can return very large responses for agencies with thousands of recipients; callers should choose smaller limits when necessary.
Recipient names can be null or replaced with 'REDACTED DUE TO PII', which can break downstream assumptions about always receiving a usable display name.
Silent acceptance of unexpected fiscal_year values (including negative or future years) can hide caller mistakes because the API returns 200 with empty results.
